principles.title: Review before mutation
principles.normalized_publication_statement: A governed review and authorization path precedes every mutation that requires human authority.

principles.title: Preserve capabilities; limit implementation scope
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Implementation sequencing may defer delivery breadth but may not erase approved architectural capabilities.

principles.title: Authorized interface parity
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Interface choice does not change authority, review, execution, provenance, or audit semantics.

principles.title: Conversation is a first-class workflow trigger
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Authorized conversational interaction may initiate governed workflows through the same durable trigger model as other interfaces.

principles.title: Optimize version 1 for trust, coherence, and daily usefulness
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Initial implementation priorities favor trustworthy, coherent, useful operation over feature count.

principles.title: Explicit ports and adapters
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Core and application behavior depend on explicit ports; infrastructure realizes those contracts through adapters.

principles.title: Preserve durable history over mutation
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Retries, cancellation, compensation, and recovery preserve prior execution history through new append-oriented records.

principles.title: Evidence precedes progress
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Progress checkpoints advance only after the evidence supporting that progress is durably stored.

principles.title: Peer interfaces share governance
principles.normalized_publication_statement: MCP remains a peer adapter over the same application services, authority, review, and audit semantics as other authorized interfaces.

principles.title: Compile governance into runtime policy
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI governance is enforceable runtime policy, not prompt text alone.

principles.title: Scope before relevance
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Authority and information scope are resolved before semantic relevance.

principles.title: Treat external content as untrusted data
principles.normalized_publication_statement: External content is evidence to inspect, not authority to obey.

principles.title: Evidence over model self-assessment
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP confidence derives from evidence and policy; model-reported confidence and hidden chain-of-thought are not authoritative records.

principles.title: One Review Service across peer interfaces
principles.normalized_publication_statement: All authorized interfaces meet at one domain-neutral Review Service and preserve identical governance and audit semantics.

principles.title: Review exact immutable versions
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Review always targets exact immutable proposal versions; revisions supersede rather than mutate reviewed content.

principles.title: Deny by default
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Security decisions begin from denial and require an explicit applicable grant.

principles.title: Network location does not confer authority
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Authentication and authorization are independent of network location.

principles.title: Separate privileged responsibilities
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Administration, migration, runtime, worker, backup, and read-only responsibilities remain separated.

principles.title: Classification follows information
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Information classification propagates into every context, derivative, log, export, and backup.

principles.title: Test by risk and invariant
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Testing depth follows architectural risk and invariants; coverage metrics support but do not replace evidence of correctness.

principles.title: Observe through provider-neutral telemetry
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP emits structured signals through provider-neutral, OpenTelemetry-compatible instrumentation.

principles.title: Define reliability as durable correctness
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Reliability is durable correctness, freshness, isolation, and recoverability rather than uptime alone.

principles.title: Degrade and reconcile explicitly
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Critical dependencies have explicit degraded behavior, and reconciliation is governed and history-preserving.

principles.title: Start production as a coherent small system
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Initial production favors one coherent application process with managed persistence and payload services, single-region and non-HA.

principles.title: Separate immutable release identity from activation
principles.normalized_publication_statement: An immutable release and Deployment Manifest do not themselves authorize capability activation.

principles.title: Design recovery across provider boundaries
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Recovery uses provider-separated evidence, isolated restores, and rehearsed procedures.

principles.title: Scale from measured need
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Vertical scaling precedes service extraction, which requires measured evidence.

principles.title: Use measurable gates instead of speculative dates
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Build sequencing is governed by evidence-bearing gates rather than speculative calendar commitments.

principles.title: Keep acceptance continuous and infrastructure earned
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Cross-cutting acceptance is continuous, and new infrastructure must be justified by measurement or a new ADR.

principles.title: Keep reproducible engineering public and authority private
principles.normalized_publication_statement: The implementation source is publicly reproducible while real private authority and runtime records remain in their governing stores.

principles.title: Dependencies point inward
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Core owns contracts, higher layers depend inward, and infrastructure implements ports under machine-enforced boundaries.

principles.title: Authoritative contracts precede generated convenience
principles.normalized_publication_statement: JSON Schema authority remains explicit, and generated language assets remain reproducible derivatives.

principles.title: Start small without bypassing governance
principles.normalized_publication_statement: The initial service footprint is minimal, but the first slice still proves the governed lifecycle.

principles.title: Use Python without becoming framework-centered
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Python provides implementation speed while canonical domain concepts remain typed and framework-independent.

principles.title: Keep model providers replaceable
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Provider-neutral contracts and deterministic test doubles preserve portability and conformance without foundational agent frameworks.

principles.title: Use one transactional foundation with distinct authority categories
principles.normalized_publication_statement: One PostgreSQL foundation may host distinct immutable, operational, and rebuildable data without collapsing their semantics or requiring universal event sourcing.

principles.title: Preserve history and exact provenance
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted truth changes through new versions and exact lineage, not destructive rewriting.

principles.title: Configuration is authority, not an override stack
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Configuration resolves through explicit authority ownership and combination rules rather than load-order precedence.

principles.title: Compile before activating
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Configuration is compiled and validated before a separate governed atomic activation.

principles.title: Combine restrictively
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Configuration combination preserves the most restrictive applicable permissions, review requirements, and ceilings.

principles.title: Treat secrets as capability-scoped references
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Secrets remain references in authority documents and are resolved only by the capability that requires them, with values excluded from durable artifacts and telemetry.

principles.title: Evidence establishes publication normativity
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Publication authority follows demonstrable approval rather than explanatory confidence or editorial inference.

principles.title: Normalize form while preserving approved meaning
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Publication normalization may improve structure and traceability but never change the authority, force, lifecycle, activation, or scope of an approved statement.

principles.title: Keep identity permanent and placement descriptive
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Stable record identity remains independent from evolving phase, subsystem, authority, and publication placement metadata.

principles.title: Preserve explanatory history and authoritative history separately
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP preserves both understandable design evolution and exact decision provenance without allowing either record to replace the other.

principles.title: Explain architecture through a problem-to-system narrative
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Architectural Foundations explains why durable, governed intelligence became necessary before presenting the technical system.

principles.title: Treat the first domain as origin and proving ground, not platform boundary
principles.normalized_publication_statement: The first domain grounds the architecture without narrowing DIP’s domain-neutral governing purpose.

principles.title: Present AI as a governed participant
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP assigns AI a bounded reasoning role while durable authority, memory, and action remain governed by the platform.

principles.title: Make the Blueprint the integrated normative architecture
principles.normalized_publication_statement: The Blueprint integrates approved architecture into a modular normative specification without replacing ADR history or machine contracts.

principles.title: Prefer precise requirements and contracts over diagram implication
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Visual models aid comprehension but cannot override normative prose, stable requirements, or exact contracts.

principles.title: Separate logical responsibility from deployment topology
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Logical authorities and responsibilities remain stable even when the initial runtime combines them into one deployment unit.

principles.title: Match decision-record weight to architectural significance
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP uses ADRs for architecturally significant choices and lighter approved records for authoritative but narrower decisions.

principles.title: Preserve decision history through explicit supersession
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Decision history remains auditable because corrections, full supersession, and partial supersession are explicit rather than silent rewrites.

principles.title: Treat an architecture release as a governing baseline
principles.normalized_publication_statement: An architecture release establishes approved design authority independently from software completion or operational certification.

principles.title: Disclose AI assistance while preserving human approval authority
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Publication provenance acknowledges AI assistance without transferring architectural approval authority away from accountable humans.

principles.title: Keep editable sources authoritative over generated editions
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Generated DOCX, PDF, tables, and archives derive from canonical editable sources and never become competing sources of truth.

principles.title: Apply classification at source and build boundaries
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Classification is enforced before rendering through separate sources and build targets, with public inputs explicitly allowlisted.

principles.title: Separate restricted architecture access from live authority access
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Architectural stewardship and operational authority are independent authorization domains.

principles.title: Authorize implementation through an exact pinned baseline
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Engineering proceeds against an explicitly pinned architecture and kickoff baseline.

principles.title: Prove governing architecture through a narrow vertical slice
principles.normalized_publication_statement: The first implementation surface remains narrow while exercising the full governed lifecycle.

principles.title: Require accountable human implementation authorization
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI may contribute implementation and evidence, but accountable human approval remains required at consequential gates.

principles.title: One logical authority per durable fact
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP assigns one accountable authority to each durable fact category.

principles.title: Authority precedes storage
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Architecture defines ownership and semantics before choosing storage products.

principles.title: Portable semantics with replaceable infrastructure
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Infrastructure may change without redefining authoritative meaning.

principles.title: Managed-first commodity infrastructure
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP minimizes commodity operating burden while retaining semantic control.

principles.title: Rebuildable derivatives do not become authority
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Derived serving structures remain replaceable and reconstructable.

principles.title: Security and authority fail closed
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Uncertainty at an authority or security boundary results in denial or stopped work.

principles.title: Technology requires evidence-based qualification
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Products are selected only after architecture-relevant evidence exists.

principles.title: Recovery includes reconciliation and isolation
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Availability restoration includes semantic and authority correctness, not only process restart.

principles.title: Human agency is the objective
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Governed AI shall improve human agency.

principles.title: Alignment is not agreement
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Goal alignment shall preserve evidence-based challenge rather than require agreement.

principles.title: Evidence precedes assertion
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Assertions shall be grounded in evaluated evidence.

principles.title: Uncertainty must be represented
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Material uncertainty shall be represented explicitly.

principles.title: Explanation must support decisions
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Explanations shall provide the information needed for governed decisions.

principles.title: DIP owns continuity
principles.normalized_publication_statement: DIP shall own durable memory and continuity.

principles.title: Context is governed access
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Context inclusion shall be treated as governed access.

principles.title: Information boundaries follow meaning
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Information boundaries shall follow semantic meaning and derived use.

principles.title: Private Strategy is separately protected
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy shall retain independent protection.

principles.title: Review precedes governed mutation
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Governed mutation shall require the applicable review and authority controls.

principles.title: Recommendations are not Commands
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Recommendations shall remain distinct from executable Commands.

principles.title: Initiative remains bounded
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI initiative shall remain explicitly bounded.

principles.title: Interfaces are governance peers
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Authorized interfaces shall preserve governance parity.

principles.title: Identity and status must be accurate
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI identity, authority, and lifecycle status shall be represented accurately.

principles.title: Interaction must remain non-deceptive
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI interaction shall not deceive users about identity, authority, evidence, or state.

principles.title: Multi-user differences remain attributable
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Multi-user goals, instructions, differences, and actions shall remain attributable.

principles.title: Failure must be visible
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Material failure shall remain visible.

principles.title: Learning is governed change
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Learning and adaptation shall be governed change.

principles.title: Models are replaceable components
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Foundation models shall remain replaceable components beneath AIOP.

principles.title: Trust requires defense in depth
principles.normalized_publication_statement: AI trust shall rely on defense-in-depth controls rather than prompt compliance alone.

principles.title: History remains intact
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Corrections, supersession, and learning shall not erase historical record.

principles.title: Outcomes complete the loop
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Governed execution shall record Outcomes and use them to complete evaluation and learning loops.

principles.title: Evidence follows its classification boundary
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Evidence shall be retained, processed, and published only within controls appropriate to its classification, with public builds isolated from restricted source contents.

principles.title: Disclose only the architectural detail necessary for the publication tier
principles.normalized_publication_statement: Each publication tier shall disclose only the architectural meaning necessary for its audience and authority boundary; completeness within the restricted tier does not create a requirement to expose restricted detail publicly.

principles.title: Omission is safer than inference or redaction
principles.normalized_publication_statement: When a public-safe projection cannot be established from approved public evidence, omit the affected field or register and record the gap; do not infer a value or redact a more complete source into apparent safety.

invariants.title: Governing hierarchy
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every AI interaction and domain operation is resolved through the ordered governing hierarchy from model to private strategic scope.

invariants.title: Stable artifact identity and immutable version identity remain separate
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Artifact identity is stable while every accepted version is immutable and independently addressable.

invariants.title: Recommendation, decision, command, and outcome remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Advisory reasoning, human or authorized decision, executable instruction, and execution result are distinct durable records.

invariants.title: Restricted inputs never enter the public build
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A public build cannot observe, load, transform, or redact Restricted Architecture, Private Authority, or Secrets.

invariants.title: Implementation remains unauthorized before IKG0
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Publication status cannot be interpreted as product implementation authorization; only IKG0 grants that authority.

invariants.title: Unit of Work ownership
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Command and workflow-transition handlers own the Unit of Work; repositories and domain objects never commit independently.

invariants.title: Durable execution hierarchy
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every governed execution resolves through Trigger → Workflow → Stage → Job → Attempt.

invariants.title: Resumption preserves pins
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Resumption retains original configuration and capability pins; reprocessing creates a distinct Workflow.

invariants.title: Analyzers cannot mutate or command
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Analyzers consume immutable accepted versions and cannot directly persist authoritative state or issue external Commands.

invariants.title: Decision, Command, and Outcome separation
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Decision Records authorize Commands, and Command execution produces append-only Outcomes; the three records never collapse into one another.

invariants.title: Accepted good state survives failed refresh
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The last accepted good state remains authoritative when a later refresh fails.

invariants.title: External effect provenance
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every external effect originates from an authorized Command and terminates in a durable Outcome, Failure, or uncertain state.

invariants.title: MCP has no privileged bypass
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: MCP cannot bypass application services or obtain direct access to databases, secrets, repositories, connectors, or model providers.

invariants.title: External authority propagation
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Actor, Workspace, applicable Private Strategy, authority, and causation remain attached to every external invocation.

invariants.title: AI governing hierarchy cannot be weakened
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every governed task resolves through Foundation Model → AIOP → Domain Package → Workspace → Private Strategy, and lower levels cannot weaken higher governance.

invariants.title: AI execution record chain
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: AI Execution Plan, Context Manifest, Prompt Package, Model Invocation, Model Result, AI Output Candidate, and Conformance Result remain first-class records.

invariants.title: AI context and output remain scope-bound
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Scope filtering precedes ranking, output visibility cannot exceed input visibility, and Private Strategy context requires explicit private scope.

invariants.title: AI tools cannot bypass application authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: AI tools are allowlisted application-query capabilities; models receive no direct infrastructure or secret access.

invariants.title: Conversation is not automatic memory
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Conversational content becomes persistent only through the governed artifact lifecycle.

invariants.title: Review binds immutable exact versions
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A Review Item binds one immutable proposal version; revisions supersede; exact-version checks precede immutable Decision Records.

invariants.title: Review disposition and execution remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Review disposition, Command creation, execution, and Outcome remain separate records and transitions.

invariants.title: Deep links are never credentials
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A deep link may identify an authorized destination but can never convey authorization.

invariants.title: Human and executing identities remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Person, Principal, Actor, Interface Client, Membership, Grant, and Delegation remain distinct, and both human initiator and executing mechanism are preserved.

invariants.title: Private Strategy authority is independent
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Workspace access and platform administration do not imply Private Strategy authority.

invariants.title: Application authorization remains primary over RLS
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Application authorization is primary and PostgreSQL RLS is mandatory defense in depth for production scope-sensitive tables.

invariants.title: Restricted encryption realms remain isolated
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Restricted payloads use envelope encryption with separately scoped security-realm keys and realm-local deduplication.

invariants.title: Security-control failure fails closed
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Identity, authorization, encryption, and key failures fail closed.

invariants.title: Security audit remains distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Security audit is distinct from diagnostic logs and domain events and remains append-oriented and content-minimized.

invariants.title: Remote production waits for security readiness
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: No production remote exposure occurs until the security readiness gate passes.

invariants.title: PostgreSQL behavior is tested on PostgreSQL
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Database mocks and SQLite never substitute for production-database behavioral and RLS tests.

invariants.title: Telemetry and authoritative records remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Telemetry, security audit, Workflow history, Event Ledger, and Conformance Reports remain distinct record classes.

invariants.title: Telemetry contains no private content or high-cardinality identities
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Telemetry fields are allowlisted, private content is prohibited, and metric labels exclude high-cardinality domain and security identities.

invariants.title: Failed work is never silently discarded
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Failed messages and artifacts remain visible and irrecoverable work enters a terminal or quarantine state.

invariants.title: Reconciliation never rewrites history silently
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Reconciliation is a governed Workflow and cannot silently rewrite authoritative history.

invariants.title: Deployment identity is immutable
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every production deployment is identified by immutable OCI image digest and Deployment Manifest.

invariants.title: Migration failure blocks deployment
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Production migrations are forward-oriented; failure blocks deployment and rollback does not rely on routine down-migration.

invariants.title: Deployment cannot activate governed capability
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Deployment and activation remain separate; deployment cannot automatically activate new capabilities, models, AIOP, or external effects.

invariants.title: MCP launch has no unauthenticated bypass
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: ChatGPT/MCP launch requires the compatibility spike, and failure leads to the authenticated fallback rather than no-auth MCP.

invariants.title: Restore evidence comes from isolated recovery
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Restores occur in isolated environments and are exercised on the approved schedule.

invariants.title: Deletion remains pending while required backups persist
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Governed deletion cannot be treated as complete while required backup copies remain in retention.

invariants.title: The first vertical slice traverses the full lifecycle
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The first vertical slice remains narrow in domain scope but traverses the complete governing lifecycle.

invariants.title: Initial governed action does not mutate the external source
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The initial Review → Decision → Command → Outcome demonstration uses an internal or no-op effect and performs no external platform mutation.

invariants.title: Private fixtures never enter public tests
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Private Workspace fixtures stay outside the public repository; public tests use synthetic structurally equivalent fixtures.

invariants.title: Market-valued output waits for qualified evidence
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Value-dependent recommendations remain disabled until qualified automated and comparison evidence satisfies the market-intelligence gate.

invariants.title: Architecture Publication precedes product implementation
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Product implementation cannot begin before Architecture Publication and the applicable authorization gate.

invariants.title: Public source never becomes private or runtime authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The public-reproducible repository does not own real Workspace or Private Strategy authority or runtime operational truth.

invariants.title: Core remains dependency-inward and application-neutral
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Core cannot depend on outer layers, and composition roots cannot become reusable canonical-logic owners.

invariants.title: Generated assets never replace authoritative schemas
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Generated models remain reproducible and drift-checked against authoritative JSON Schemas.

invariants.title: Canonical domain models remain framework-independent
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, and provider SDK objects never become canonical domain authority.

invariants.title: Stable identity and immutable version identity remain separate
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Artifact identity remains stable while accepted versions are immutable and corrections create new versions.

invariants.title: Recorded, observed, accepted, and effective time remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Persisted temporal facts retain timezone-aware UTC and declared temporal meaning.

invariants.title: Acceptance is atomic
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Artifact acceptance and its required provenance, lifecycle, and outbox records commit together or not at all.

invariants.title: Payload presence does not confer acceptance authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Only governed database references authorize payload acceptance; blob writes precede acceptance and unreferenced objects remain removable.

invariants.title: Retries never overwrite Attempt history
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Workflow records are durable and every retry creates a new Attempt.

invariants.title: Review, Decision, Recommendation, Command, and Outcome remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Governed advisory, authority, execution, and result records never collapse into one another.

invariants.title: Application use cases own transactions
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Domain objects and repositories never commit independently, and long external I/O stays outside the Unit of Work.

invariants.title: Propagation is outbox-backed and replay-safe
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Asynchronous propagation uses transactional outbox, deduplication, and explicit idempotency keys.

invariants.title: Authoritative rows carry explicit scope
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Workspace and applicable Private Strategy scope are explicit and receive defense-in-depth isolation before shared production.

invariants.title: Governed artifacts are not destructively deleted
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Authoritative history is preserved while rebuildable projections may be removed and regenerated.

invariants.title: Configuration authority cannot expand silently
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Unauthorized values fail compilation, and lower or runtime layers can only narrow capability.

invariants.title: Validation never implies activation
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A candidate becomes authoritative only through atomic activation, and failure preserves the prior valid activation.

invariants.title: Execution remains pinned to activation history
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Workflows and governed decision/execution records retain exact configuration activation references.

invariants.title: Secret values never enter authoritative or generated records
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Only typed references enter configuration; resolved values never enter bundles, logs, events, prompts, artifacts, or exceptions.

invariants.title: Private Strategy never falls back, combines, or broadens visibility implicitly
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy requires explicit scoped authority, narrows output visibility, and cannot be inferred from Workspace or operator authority.

invariants.title: Configuration failure preserves last valid authority and remains visible
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Invalid candidates or missing sources do not invent replacement authority; permitted degradation is bounded, freshness visible, and provenance explainable.

invariants.title: Inference never becomes normative publication authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: No inferred statement may enter the normative publication inventory.

invariants.title: Existing publication and ADR identifiers never change
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Existing stable identifiers remain unchanged across normalization, movement, and publication composition.

invariants.title: Every normalized decision retains both wording layers
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A normalized decision is incomplete unless both its approved-source and architectural-publication statements are retained.

invariants.title: Editorial normalization never changes approved semantics
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Normalization preserves obligation, approval, modality, lifecycle, activation, history, and vendor or implementation scope.

invariants.title: Deferred and conditional states retain their approved meaning
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Deferred capability remains governed architecture outside current scope, and conditional capability remains inactive until its approved conditions are satisfied.

invariants.title: P0 planning approval does not pass PG0
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Approval of the P0 plan defines PG0 but cannot pass it; PG0 passage requires verified satisfaction of every approved blocking criterion.

invariants.title: Foundations never expose real private instances or data
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Public Foundations contain no real Workspace, Private Strategy, membership, roster, or manager instance data.

invariants.title: Foundations do not replace normative technical authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Foundations may explain and summarize but cannot replace normative Blueprint requirements, contracts, or ADRs.

invariants.title: Every governing architecture area remains represented
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The Blueprint cannot omit governing architecture merely because a capability is conditional, deferred, or outside the initial runtime.

invariants.title: Every verifiable obligation has stable identity
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Verifiable Blueprint obligations retain permanent identifiers across document movement and release evolution.

invariants.title: Blueprint changes remain atomic across dependent records
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A semantic change cannot update one architectural representation while leaving dependent representations inconsistent.

invariants.title: Existing identifiers and gaps are preserved
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Published identifiers are never renumbered, reused, or silently filled.

invariants.title: Accepted decision bodies are substantively immutable
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Semantic decision change requires a new record and explicit supersession rather than in-place rewriting.

invariants.title: Public decisions exclude private approval evidence
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Public decision records remain understandable without exposing private evidence locators, conversations, or operational data.

invariants.title: Approved releases and letters remain immutable
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: An approved architecture release and its Architect’s Letter remain frozen historical baselines.

invariants.title: Checksums do not establish authorship
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Checksums prove content integrity only and cannot be represented as proof of human authorship or approval.

invariants.title: Generated files never become independent sources
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every semantic correction returns to canonical source and is regenerated through the controlled toolchain.

invariants.title: The public build never consumes restricted input
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Public production loads only explicitly allowlisted public sources and never loads restricted material for later redaction.

invariants.title: Secrets appear in no publication artifact or derivative
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Secret values are absent from sources, generated files, workspaces, logs, archives, backups, and recovery copies.

invariants.title: No product implementation coding occurs before IKG0
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Until a separate human authorization grants IKG0, work is limited to approved publication, planning, review, validation, and non-code evidence activities.

invariants.title: Implementation contradictions fail closed
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Affected work stops until the governing state is unambiguous; implementation convenience cannot become de facto architecture.

invariants.title: Gate approval cannot be self-certified by AI
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: AI contributions require independent evidence and accountable approval at the applicable gate.

invariants.title: Information lifecycle order is preserved
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Receive, Validate, Accept, Version, Propagate, and Observe remain distinct ordered states.

invariants.title: Workflow hierarchy order is preserved
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Trigger, Workflow, Stage, Job, and Attempt remain distinct ordered execution identities.

invariants.title: Semantic message families never collapse
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Domain Events, Orchestration Signals, Commands, and Outcomes remain distinct.

invariants.title: Artifact representation classes never collapse
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Raw, Normalized, Canonical, and Derived artifacts remain distinct.

invariants.title: Accepted authority is immutable
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Accepted authoritative records are immutable; mutable current state is revision-controlled.

invariants.title: Every durable transition has one commit owner
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: One declared authority owns each durable state transition.

invariants.title: Derivatives hold no exclusive authoritative fact
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A projection, index, cache, replica, or generated view never holds the only authoritative fact.

invariants.title: Delivery stages remain separately governed
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Source, build, release, deployment, migration, promotion, and activation remain distinct.

invariants.title: First-domain semantics never enter Core
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: First-domain vocabulary and behavior remain in the Domain Package, not DIP Core.

invariants.title: Real operational Workspace identities remain restricted
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Public architecture uses abstract Workspace boundaries and does not disclose real operational identities.

invariants.title: Operational and portability Workspaces remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The first operational Workspace and portability Workspace are separate governed boundaries.

invariants.title: G1 and readiness approval remain distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: G1 completion does not substitute for the separate First-Domain Viability & Implementation Readiness Review.

invariants.title: Readiness review remains distinct
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: G1 completion does not substitute for the First-Domain Viability & Implementation Readiness Review.

invariants.title: AIOP precedes implementation planning
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Implementation planning does not begin before AIOP completion and approval.

invariants.title: Initial surface may shrink but architecture may not
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Version 1 scope discipline reduces delivery surface without reducing the governing architecture.

invariants.title: Lower AI layers cannot weaken higher controls
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Domain, Workspace, Private Strategy, or task specialization cannot weaken AIOP constitutional controls.

invariants.title: Prompts and confidence confer no authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: No prompt, retrieved content, or model confidence score grants authority.

invariants.title: Recommendations never become Commands directly
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A Recommendation cannot become an executable Command without a governed Decision Record and authority path.

invariants.title: Conversation is not durable memory
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Conversation or session state cannot silently become accepted durable memory.

invariants.title: Context cannot cross scope without authorization
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Context cannot enter or cross a governed scope without explicit authorization and provenance.

invariants.title: Private Strategy remains independently protected
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Private Strategy protection cannot be reduced to Workspace membership alone.

invariants.title: Initiative is not authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Observe, Analyze, Propose, or Prepare capability does not imply Execute authority.

invariants.title: Review precedes governed mutation
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Consequential governed mutation cannot bypass applicable Review Service and authority controls.

invariants.title: Interfaces do not change governance
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Changing an authorized interface cannot change the meaning, authority, review, or audit behavior of an action.

invariants.title: Interaction status reflects actual state
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: AI interaction cannot claim approval, execution, completion, or certainty that the governed records do not support.

invariants.title: Material failure is not hidden
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Protected-boundary, consequential-action, and uncertain-execution failures cannot be silently suppressed.

invariants.title: Uncertain external effects are reconciled
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: An uncertain external effect cannot be blindly retried before reconciliation.

invariants.title: Learning cannot silently change governing authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Learning and adaptation cannot silently modify the Constitution, AIOP policy, authority, or accepted memory.

invariants.title: Fallback must remain conforming and visible
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Model or provider fallback cannot occur unless the replacement deployment is qualified and the substitution is visible.

invariants.title: Conformance applies to a deployment combination
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A model alone cannot be declared globally DIP-conformant.

invariants.title: The model is not a durable authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: No foundation model or provider deployment can become DIP's system of record, durable memory authority, authorization authority, or workflow authority.

invariants.title: Durable continuity remains outside model state
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: DIP identity, knowledge, memory, provenance, temporal state, and audit history cannot depend on continuation of a particular model session or provider.

invariants.title: Lower specialization cannot weaken higher governance
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Domain, Workspace, Private Strategy, or task specialization cannot silently weaken higher AIOP or DIP Core controls.

invariants.title: Companion intelligence does not imply autonomous authority
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: The companion relationship cannot confer implicit authority to promote memory, approve decisions, mutate state, issue Commands, or represent a user externally.

invariants.title: Public builds never consume restricted evidence
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: No public build step may read, import, transform, or redact restricted source contents.

invariants.title: Complete account exports never become architecture source
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Only reviewed, allowlisted DIP conversation packages may enter restricted evidence control; the complete account export may not persist in either architecture repository.

invariants.title: Architecture repositories never become Private Authority or secret stores
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Neither the public nor restricted architecture repository may contain real instance state, active private configuration, or secret values, or acquire authority over them.

invariants.title: Public ADR projections neither consume restricted bodies nor reveal protected-instance existence
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: A public ADR projection shall be generated only from its explicit public inputs, shall never read the restricted ADR body, and shall neither confirm nor deny whether any real protected instance exists.

invariants.title: Public structured projections accept no undeclared fields
invariants.normalized_publication_statement: Every public structured-register projection and every projected record shall use a closed schema shape; source-evidence, authority, restricted-location, Private Authority, and secret-bearing fields can never become valid through extension or pass-through behavior.
